summary: Guessed eval cell cost from intuition (~$0.40) instead of reading prior run data; off by ~50x (actual ~$22.76)
preceding_cue: About to state a cost estimate and confirm before launching an engram eval or LLM-harness run
lesson: Derive eval-spend estimates from real per-cell/per-trial costs (read prior run's result JSON or RESULTS.md), multiply by fan-out; never guess from intuition

summary: Planned to answer a retrieval-capability generalization question with one expensive end-to-end LLM run without first checking if the stressor even competes
preceding_cue: About to design or launch an eval asking whether memory/retrieval capability survives a stressor (crowding, scale, competition)
lesson: Run a free Tier-1 local retrieval probe first (sweep stressor, check surfaced+rank) as a realism gate before any paid Tier-2 spend; a flat Tier-1 curve answers retrieval and reframes the paid run

summary: Started designing a payload restructure without verifying where the bulk actually lived; conflated the goal with a specific tactic and nearly mislabeled a smaller lever as the big one
preceding_cue: About to act on a 'restructure / make it more efficient' or payload-reduction lever whose win location is assumed not measured
lesson: First verify where the bulk actually is (free engram query measurement); the biggest safe reducer is often a differently-labeled smaller item — do it first, measure, then decide if the riskier restructure is worth it

summary: Ran `targ build` to install the engram binary (target does not exist); stale binary on PATH caused measurements to read as unchanged, nearly mis-concluding a change did nothing
preceding_cue: About to rebuild or reinstall the engram binary to verify a CLI/query/output change
lesson: Install the product binary with `go install ./cmd/engram`; `targ` covers test/lint/check only — there is no `targ build` target

summary: Treated a deterministic payload-size cut as sufficient evidence of a win and was about to ship lazy/deferred retrieval without measuring whether the agent actually fetches sparsely and correctly
preceding_cue: About to ship or recommend a lazy/on-demand/two-speed retrieval mode where bulk content is deferred and fetched on demand
lesson: Before shipping, measure agent's natural uninstructed fetch behavior: (a) SPARING — fetch count must stay well under break-even N; (b) CAPABLE — sole-source fixture proves agent fetches the right deferred item unprompted; a size delta alone does not establish a net win

summary: Listed async/non-blocking learn as an efficiency lever on a cost/tokens/time roadmap; it only relocates cost, not reduces it
preceding_cue: About to propose or rank 'async / background / deferred / non-blocking' work as an efficiency improvement on a cost, tokens, or time axis
lesson: Async/background/deferred relocates cost — same tokens, same dollars, same total wall-time; classify as perceived-latency UX and exclude from cost/time efficiency roadmaps

summary: Replaced structured vault with flat lessons.md and left clustering/dedup to agent judgment, discarding k-means and cosine-band deterministic machinery
preceding_cue: About to simplify or redesign a memory/data system by replacing structured components with flat files or agent improvisation
lesson: Re-host existing deterministic machinery (clustering, dedup rules, ID schemes) in the new design; flat files and eyeballed grouping are red flags for regressing scaling properties

summary: Treated uncommitted working-tree deletions as accidental data loss and git-restored them without asking; user had deliberately deleted the files
preceding_cue: About to run git restore / git checkout on deleted or changed files that were not the result of own actions
lesson: Surface what changed and ask whether it was intentional before restoring anything; unexplained deletions are not automatically damage

summary: Asked user to ratify a synthesis that existed only as chat-table summaries with no reviewable artifact; user could not distinguish new decisions from echoed answers
preceding_cue: About to ask user to approve, ratify, or review a design, plan, or synthesis
lesson: Write the full synthesis to a reviewable artifact first (decisions split into user-decided vs agent-proposed), then ask for review of that artifact — never ask for approval of fragments that exist only in chat

summary: Scoped a discipline-enforcement skill to TEACH the technique; RED baseline did not reproduce because capable models already apply it unprompted
preceding_cue: About to design or validate a skill aimed at capable models for a 'do it the right way' discipline (TDD rigor, review, testing technique)
lesson: Scope the skill to ENFORCE under pressure (model being rushed or told by authority it's overkill), not teach; build RED from pressure scenarios; make the spine a non-waivable mandate

summary: Collapsed a requested set of N diverse options to a smaller recommended subset, removing the very diversity the user wanted to evaluate against
preceding_cue: User asks for N diverse options, approaches, or pitches to evaluate
lesson: Deliver the full N as distinct independently-evaluable options with honest per-option ratings (contender/park); make the recommendation a position inside the set, never by deleting members

summary: Applied recency multiplier and fillRecencyBand on []resolvedItem after type conversion, causing a type error or silent no-op because resolvedItem lacks cosine score fields
preceding_cue: About to wire a recency re-rank or adaptive band into a query pipeline that converts scoredChunk to resolvedItem
lesson: applyChunkRecency + sortScoredDesc must operate on []scoredChunk BEFORE the item-build loop; fillRecencyBand operates on already-converted []resolvedItem post-cap

summary: Applied note recency decay to scoredCandidate after final sort without adding baseScore field; activation cutoff then used decayed score instead of raw score
preceding_cue: About to wire note recency decay into the note-ranking pipeline (rankCandidates / scoredCandidate)
lesson: Add baseScore float32 to scoredCandidate; multiply score by recencyMultiplier in rankCandidates before sort; pass now time.Time into rankCandidates and thread deps.Now() from callers

summary: Left struct fields unused for a later task scope; unused linter fired and nolint suppression was attempted rather than adding a minimal consumer
preceding_cue: About to commit a struct with fields that have no consumer yet because their consumer belongs to a later planned task
lesson: Add a minimal consumer (e.g. defaultRecencyParams() reading all fields) in the same commit scope so the unused linter is satisfied without nolint suppression

summary: Assumed RunQuery could acquire vault flock from LearnDeps.Lock for a new sidecar write side-effect; QueryDeps has no Lock field
preceding_cue: About to add a vault write side-effect (--activate, bumpLastUsed) to RunQuery
lesson: QueryDeps has no Lock field; add Lock or WriteSidecar-that-locks to QueryDeps and wire it, or call bumpLastUsed without the vault flock (sidecar writes are single-file atomic via os.WriteFile)

summary: Set all distractors to 90d in a recency eval; band guarantee made plantedRank=0 in every half-life cell, providing no discrimination — half-life choice was arbitrary
preceding_cue: About to build a recency eval with a planted very-recent chunk and a pool of distractor chunks
lesson: Spread distractors across realistic ages (0.5d, 2d, 5d, 15d, 60d) and add a mid-age probe whose rank changes across half-life cells; that change is the discrimination signal

summary: Introduced a new outer variable to lift an inner-block value past its scope; trapped in a funlen + wsl_v5 cycle (blank line demanded by wsl → funlen fails; removal → wsl fails)
preceding_cue: About to introduce a new outer variable to lift a value from an inner if-block to an outer call site in a file where funlen and wsl_v5 are active
lesson: Use the value expression inline at the call site (e.g. defaultRecencyParams().floor) — no new variable, no blank-line tension, funlen count unchanged; only introduce outer var when needed at multiple sites

summary: Wrote a half-life sweep eval with all distractors uniformly 90d old; band guarantee gave plantedRank=0 everywhere and the 'tuning' claim was false
preceding_cue: About to write a half-life sweep eval with a planted recent chunk and a uniform distractor pool
lesson: Spread distractors across realistic age tiers; add a mid-age probe (age > windowDays) whose rank changes across half-life cells; assert the chosen hl surfaces the probe and a too-long hl does not

summary: Picked time-decay/window constants assuming a daily work rhythm and baked that timescale into eval data without asking the user's actual cadence
preceding_cue: Agent is about to propose or write a time-decay half-life, recency window, or threshold constant value
lesson: Ask the user's actual work cadence BEFORE setting time-based defaults; calibrate both constants and eval age scales to the real cadence, never hard-code an assumed timescale

summary: Declared a path-taking CLI subcommand done after unit tests + no-arg deploy check, without running the real binary with real path args from a non-data cwd; a cwd-relative path bug shipped
preceding_cue: Agent is about to declare a CLI subcommand done after unit tests pass where the command does filesystem I/O on path arguments
lesson: Before declaring a path-taking CLI command done, run the real installed binary with real path args from a cwd that is NOT the data dir; in-memory tests and no-arg no-ops cannot catch cwd/vault path-resolution bugs

summary: Used bytes.Join after bytes.Split to strip a trailing section, dropping the trailing newline when no blank line precedes the marker, causing a hash mismatch
preceding_cue: Agent is about to write bytes.Join(parts, sep) after bytes.Split to strip a trailing section from a newline-terminated body
lesson: After bytes.Join, check if the result ends with newline and append one if not: if len(result) > 0 && result[len(result)-1] != newline { result = append(result, newline) }

summary: Wrote a classical backward loop (for i := len(s)-1; i >= 0; i--) in engram Go code; the modernize linter rejects it and targ check-full fails
preceding_cue: Agent is writing a backward iteration loop over a slice in engram Go code
lesson: Use slices.Backward: for i, elem := range slices.Backward(slice) { ... } and import slices

summary: Indexed into a bytes.Split result without a nil guard; nilaway reported a potential nil panic and targ check-full failed
preceding_cue: Agent is about to index or slice into the return value of bytes.Split in engram Go code
lesson: Add if lines == nil { return body } guard immediately after bytes.Split before any indexing

summary: Defaulted to conservative cleanup (keep historical docs, cautious branch pruning) when this user repeatedly overrode toward decisive removal
preceding_cue: User asks to clean up stale artifacts, branches, historical docs, or subcommands
lesson: Lean aggressive on this user's cleanup asks — consolidate to ONE concise doc then delete the rest, prune abandoned branches, delete stale artifacts — using git history as recovery net; confirm scope once then execute decisively

summary: Accepted a reviewer's claim that a numbered design decision was reversed without reading the decision's actual wording; the claim was false and the decision's scope did not cover what the reviewer alleged
preceding_cue: A reviewer subagent says 'decision D[N] is reversed/violated by this change'
lesson: Before accepting a reversal claim, read the decision's actual wording/scope at source (DESIGN-HISTORY/ADR); reviewers over-read decisions — verify, then accept or rebut

summary: Trusted stale docs describing removed mechanisms (3-hop subgraph, tier filters) as current; agents designed probes for dead code paths, causing material misses
preceding_cue: Agent is designing a probe, feature, or eval for a system that recently had a major mechanism removed or replaced
lesson: After any major refactor or feature removal, immediately audit docs, auto-memory, and skills for references to the removed mechanism; stale references compound into design misses

summary: Trusted docs describing the recall mechanism (a 3-hop subgraph) and designed a C6 eval probe around it; the described path was vestigial and never called by the shipping skill
preceding_cue: Agent is about to design an eval, probe, or feature based on a documented mechanism without tracing the real entry-point code
lesson: Trace the real call chain from the entry point before designing against a mechanism; docs/memory/comments are hints to verify, never ground truth

summary: Treated code removal as done once it compiled/passed, leaving stale docs, memory, glossary, and diagrams that actively misled future agents
preceding_cue: Agent declares a code-path, flag, or architecture removal done after code compiles and tests pass
lesson: A removal is not done until every doc, memory note, glossary entry, and diagram naming the old path is updated or deleted in the same effort; run a doc+memory+diagram staleness sweep as part of the removal

summary: Swept obvious call sites of a removed flag/subcommand but skipped running downstream consumer self-checks (validate.py), leaving the eval harness more broken than before
preceding_cue: Agent finishes updating the obvious call sites of a removed flag/subcommand and is about to declare the cross-cutting removal done
lesson: Treat test/eval harnesses as first-class consumers of any cross-cutting removal: enumerate every invocation, fix them all, and run the consumer's self-check to green before calling the removal done

summary: Treated user corrections as one-off fixes and over-thought trap taxonomy instead of capturing each correction as a reproducible test condition
preceding_cue: User issues a correction to something the agent said or did
lesson: Treat every correction as something to make non-recurring: capture the concrete triggering condition so it can be reproduced as a cheap test/guard; goal is reproducibility, not classifying the trap

summary: Treated within-cluster aggregation of co-topical notes as emergent synthesis; built C6 test fixtures that measure what cosine clustering already does natively, missing that real compositional synthesis requires a relational graph
preceding_cue: Agent is designing eval fixtures labeled 'synthesis' or 'emergent synthesis' for a vector-retrieval memory system
lesson: Distinguish aggregation/abstraction (similarity-based, what cosine+k-means does) from compositional/transitive/analogical synthesis (relational, requires a graph); engram's internal/vaultgraph wikilink graph is the substrate for the latter

summary: Framed analogy as a peer inference mode that justifies creating links alongside deduction/abduction; user corrected that analogy is not truth-preserving and must be a generation step, not a persistence step
preceding_cue: Agent is designing a mechanism that uses analogy or 'what else is like this' to connect or infer new links
lesson: Analogy is a GENERATION/path-finding step (propose candidates, persist nothing); only persist a link after a truth-preserving mode (deduction/abduction/composition) validates it

summary: Assumed the RED baseline would confirm the design's premise (0 cross-links) and planned to engineer fixtures to reproduce it, rather than treating the contradictory measurement as a finding that falsified the premise
preceding_cue: A TDD RED baseline produces results that contradict the design's assumed failure mode
lesson: When the RED baseline contradicts the design premise, stop and surface it with the measured reality; reframe the spec rather than re-engineering the test to fit the assumption; verify the premise empirically before building on it

summary: Accepted a confident reviewer blocking claim ('engram amend writes frontmatter, BFS won't see slice-1 edges') as ground truth and began reworking the approach; the claim was wrong when checked against the code
preceding_cue: An adversarial reviewer subagent raises a BLOCKING claim that would force a redesign
lesson: Verify a reviewer's blocking claim against the actual code/runtime the same way you'd verify your own; a blocking finding is a hypothesis to confirm, not a fact — run it and read the output before redesigning

summary: Validated graph-expanded retrieval with a single bare engram query; real multi-phrase A/B showed zero marginal value because the recall skill's 10 broad phrases already subsume graph traversal
preceding_cue: Agent is about to validate a retrieval improvement layer using a single bare engram query call rather than the real skill's multi-phrase recall
lesson: Validate any retrieval layer through the real skill's multi-phrase recall, not a single bare engram query; a marginal-value A/B (layer on vs off) is mandatory before claiming a retrieval layer adds value

summary: Assumed engram needs a reasoning/retrieval scaffold (graph expansion, synthesis step, multi-hop) to improve recall quality and started building it; three consecutive A/B negatives showed the agent already reasons excellently given the right notes
preceding_cue: About to propose or build a graph expansion, synthesis layer, multi-hop retrieval, or reranking mechanism for engram to 'improve recall quality'
lesson: Engram's value is the memory itself; opus already does reasoning spontaneously. Default to NO reasoning/retrieval scaffold; require warm-only-vs-warm+scaffold A/B showing real headroom first — usually there is none

summary: Ran a one-shot in-session eval (opus composes A+B->C in its answer, 18/18), concluded synthesis capability was redundant, conflating transient in-session reasoning with durable persisted synthesis
preceding_cue: About to conclude a capability is redundant because a one-shot eval shows the agent already produces the conclusion in its answer
lesson: Separate in-session composition (no scaffold needed — proven redundant) from PERSISTING synthesis (distinct write-side capability, the compounding lever); test with multi-session laddering A/B, not a one-shot answer check

summary: Built a depth-parameterized chain fixture where the terminal was a literal fact stored verbatim, making it a lookup-and-follow task instead of synthesis; the no-persist arm trivially scored 100%, producing a false negative
preceding_cue: Designing an eval fixture to test synthesis/composition/compounding reasoning, optimizing for a clean deterministic terminal-token metric
lesson: Synthesis-eval fixtures must require an EMERGENT conclusion stated in NO note (A+B->C genuinely new); verify the baseline arm must derive the conclusion; prefer LLM judge over token match when a clean token would force a degenerate literal-terminal fixture

summary: Evaluated persisting synthesis only by whether it improves strong-model task accuracy, found it redundant (opus re-derives fine), nearly dropped it; missed inspection, correction, and weaker-model reuse values
preceding_cue: Evaluating whether to persist synthesis conclusions by measuring only strong-model task accuracy improvement
lesson: Persist sound reasoned conclusions as synthesis notes: accuracy is the wrong yardstick; the value is inspection (auditable), correction (can only fix what exists as a note), and reuse by weaker models. Gate against vault-rot: only sound conclusions, linked to inputs

summary: Framed cheaper-model delegation for part of an agent workflow as building bespoke split infrastructure or delegating the whole skill wholesale to the cheap model
preceding_cue: About to design or build infrastructure for delegating part of an agent skill to a cheaper model
lesson: Split the SKILL at its judgment seam — host (strong model) keeps reasoning needing its context; sub-skill on cheap model does mechanical work and returns raw material. Use existing subagent model-override; no new infra needed

summary: Built the host/haiku recall split via TDD; it worked quality-wise but only cut op cost ~14%, making the complexity not worth it — should have measured op-level delta before building
preceding_cue: About to split a skill across models for cost savings without first measuring the delegated part's share of overall op cost
lesson: Don't split a skill across models for cost unless the delegated part is a LARGE share of op cost; measure the op-level delta first before building the split

summary: Re-proposed cheaper-model-tier recall as a fresh cost lever despite the in-context EXPERIMENT-LOG showing it was already tried, measured at -14%/op, and rolled back as not worth it
preceding_cue: About to recommend a cost or performance lever / optimization approach after an eval series
lesson: Before recommending any lever, actively check whether it was already tried and rejected (query lever name + 'rolled back'/'not worth it'); in-context presence is necessary-not-sufficient — synthesis must explicitly reconcile each option against prior lever OUTCOMES

summary: Scored only the end recommendation for respecting a known-failed option, conflating retrieval-failure (evidence never surfaced) with synthesis-failure (evidence in-context but ignored); a synthesis-only fix could pass falsely GREEN
preceding_cue: Designing an eval/metric for whether an agent respects prior knowledge, using only the end recommendation as the signal
lesson: Pair the end metric with a diagnostic sub-metric ('did the disproving evidence surface in recall at all?'); ensure >=1 fixture reproduces the RETRIEVAL miss (note buried/absent), not just the synthesis miss; confirm current skill scores ~0.0 RED

summary: Said 'recall+learn is the expense' (wall-time) and 'the build is the dollar sink' (absolute spend) as if they answered the same question, conflating two independent axes and two different goals
preceding_cue: Reporting where time/cost goes across phases of a multi-phase operation (e.g. recall+build+learn)
lesson: Keep wall-time and dollar axes separate; separate the MARGINAL warm-over-cold premium from ABSOLUTE spend; check whether phases share a meter (a phase bundled inside another's session is an inference until unbundled)

summary: About to either author hundreds of notes to reproduce a 'buried at scale' eval condition or declare it irreproducible, rather than using a stub binary
preceding_cue: Eval needs a 'note present but not surfaced by retrieval at scale' condition that a small fixture cannot naturally reproduce
lesson: Put a stub binary on PATH that reproduces the MEASURED retrieval behavior deterministically and logs calls; this fakes scale cheaply, makes the gap a deterministic mechanism test; verify stub against a real measurement first so it encodes reality

summary: During /please on issue 655, pivoted mid-task to recommending the deferred 654 work as 'the real solve' without flagging the reversal — a displacement treadmill that ignored the user's explicit task assignment
preceding_cue: About to recommend, challenge, or re-scope a course of action — especially when synthesizing a recommendation late in a task with prior conclusions/decisions already in context
lesson: Before any recommendation or challenge, explicitly reconcile against recent history: name what was already concluded/decided; if reversing, SAY you are reversing and justify with a NAMED NEW fact. When user says 'do X', default to doing X

summary: Vague 'reconcile before recommending / do not displace' instruction only halved failure (100%->50%) because agents re-rationalize displacement as diligence ('prerequisite / dependency order'), which doesn't feel like relitigating
preceding_cue: Writing an instruction or rule to stop an agent from displacing the asked task with prerequisite/verification/foundation work
lesson: Name the exact self-deception: 'substituting prerequisite/verification/foundation work FEELS like diligence but IS relitigating the settled task'; frame the asked task as a SETTLED decision to BEGIN this turn; deviation allowed only on a genuinely NEW fact + explicit acknowledgement of reversing

summary: Followed a chain of individually-plausible tasks (cost investigation -> recall miss -> C7 harness -> recall fixes) several steps off the stated product goal and proposed continuing those threads as 'next' without re-validating necessity
preceding_cue: About to carry prior plan items forward as 'still necessary' or propose the next investigation thread in a multi-step task chain
lesson: Periodically RE-ANCHOR on the stated product goal; before doing carried-forward work, re-validate FRESH that it is necessary FOR THE GOAL (a past 'this might help' is not validation); name plainly when a thread is off-goal or cost-neutral, then return to the goal's real lever

summary: Asserted a fundamental 'cannot' (memory can't make builds cheaper or faster) from a single contingent measurement by collapsing wall-time and dollar axes into one verdict — the dollar axis actually allowed cheaper builds
preceding_cue: About to assert a capability is impossible ('X cannot be cheaper/faster/etc.') or draw a net conclusion across multiple cost/perf axes
lesson: Before asserting 'X cannot', DECOMPOSE by axis and judge each separately; distinguish STRUCTURAL limits (true by arithmetic) from CONTINGENT measurements (one n=5 result); a limit on one axis is not a limit on all

summary: Took the field name 'recall_s'=350s at face value and built load-bearing conclusions ('recall overhead swamps the build', 'faster is structurally impossible') across multiple sessions; it actually timed round-1 (recall+build), real recall was ~190s
preceding_cue: About to cite a named metric/field/log value to support a load-bearing conclusion
lesson: Before building any conclusion on a named metric, VERIFY what it actually measures — read the code/definition that produces it (what window/rows/units), not just its name. A field named X is a hint, not proof it measures X — especially when the number is surprising or load-bearing

summary: Reported favorable nuances (amortization paying off, build time tied) before adversarial verification finished; verification refuted both — pooled -45s saving collapsed to -5.5s noise when properly paired
preceding_cue: About to report a favorable/hoped-for nuance from an eval (amortization pays off, consuming case wins, it's a tie) before adversarial verification is complete
lesson: Verify BEFORE reporting any favorable nuance: (1) pair/match before pooling; (2) a gap below noise is UNDERPOWERED, never a 'tie'; (3) size every delta against the same-contrast spread; (4) be most skeptical of the result you or the user WANT to be true

summary: Predicted cold-fail from gotcha plausibility on paper; ~half of hand-picked idiosyncratic conventions saturated (opus applied them cold 3-5/5), and all 4 behavioral/conceptual traps dissolved 0/5 in clean toy context
preceding_cue: About to classify eval gotchas as 'cold fail' cases and bless calibration based on paper plausibility without empirical pilot runs
lesson: Gate every per-gotcha cold-fail claim behind 1-2 trial cold pilots BEFORE warm spend; score gotchas individually not per-app; favor token-level tactical habits (fire 5/5), distrust behavioral/conceptual ones (0/5 in clean toy context)

summary: Assistant recommended cost optimizations without recalling that cheaper-tier for the mechanical recall half had already been tried and failed in a prior session
preceding_cue: assistant presenting 'memory works the way you think' summary and listing procedure optimization directions
lesson: Before recommending cheaper-tier splits for recall, recall that the cheaper tier for the mechanical half was already tried and found to be a failure

summary: Assistant gave contradictory accounts in the same session about where the cost lives: first 'build is NOT the expense, recall+learn is', then 'recall is only a sub-share, pay attention to the build loop'
preceding_cue: assistant presenting multi-step cost analysis after completing a workflow
lesson: Maintain strict internal consistency on cost-bottleneck claims across a session; don't flip between 'build is the bottleneck' and 'recall+learn is the bottleneck' without explicit reconciliation

summary: Assistant told the user 'synthesis is presented' but had only delivered summaries and bullet points; user had to ask where the synthesis document was
preceding_cue: assistant presenting conclusions from a brainstorm/planning step and asking for ratification
lesson: Do not present bullet-point summaries as synthesis; synthesis is a new artifact that combines sources into something not present in any single source — deliver it before claiming it exists

summary: Assistant gave surface-level branch recommendations without reading session logs to determine whether branches were intentionally left for future work or should have been dropped
preceding_cue: assistant analyzing worktree branches and providing disposition recommendations
lesson: When asked to analyze branches from working sessions, read the actual session logs to understand intentionality before making recommendations; the session context is available and must be used

summary: Test suite verified that show-chunk mechanism works technically, but no test checked that the agent surfaces a chunk without being explicitly instructed to do so
preceding_cue: assistant reporting feature built with TDD and recall skill fixed, about to declare complete
lesson: For features that rely on agent behavior (e.g., show-chunk), require a test that the agent uses the feature unprompted; a mechanism-only test misses the actual value proposition

summary: Assistant implemented async learn as a cost/performance optimization, but async reduces only wall-clock time — not tokens or cost — which were the user's actual concerns
preceding_cue: assistant shipping async learn as a performance optimization in the binary and skill
lesson: Async learn reduces wall-clock time only, not tokens or cost; when the user's concern is tokens/cost, async is not the right lever

summary: Assistant leaned on existing harness results when recommending next steps, but those results were from an old architecture and do not bind the new one
preceding_cue: assistant summarizing harness map and recommending next evaluation steps after an architecture change
lesson: Stale eval results from a prior architecture do not bind a new architecture; when the system under test has changed, re-validate fresh and do not cite old findings as current

summary: Assistant presented eval results as prose/partial table without labeled columns with units, forcing the user to ask again — a pattern the user has had to correct repeatedly
preceding_cue: assistant completing an eval run and presenting cold-to-warm results
lesson: Always lead eval results with a labeled criteria table: columns with units mapped to C1/C2/C3/C4-C6, arms side by side with deltas; never narrate numbers in prose or use bare %/'cut'

summary: Assistant described 'emergent synthesis' as ordering or conjunction-gluing of items; user corrected that emergent synthesis means A and B each have independent clusters and C emerges when a query unexpectedly bridges them
preceding_cue: assistant proposing synthesis fixture patterns for the eval harness
lesson: Emergent synthesis = A and B each have their own independent clusters; C emerges when a query unexpectedly joins them; it is not ordering and not conjunction-gluing of unrelated items

summary: Assistant recommended implementing graph RAG despite its own analysis showing it underperforms naive read; user had to point out the logical inconsistency
preceding_cue: assistant delivering a /please analysis with a recommendation to implement graph RAG
lesson: Do not recommend implementing an approach when your own analysis shows it underperforms the naive baseline; present the data and let the user decide

summary: Assistant pursued complex third-party datasets for abduction/deduction/induction testing when the user wanted simple self-authored markdown examples with 5 samples each
preceding_cue: assistant investigating synthesis failure mechanisms using third-party data sources
lesson: For reasoning pattern tests (abduction/deduction/induction), use simple self-authored markdown with 5 clear examples each rather than third-party systems or datasets

summary: Assistant proposed a meatier build when the user's insight was to split recall into two skills: opus-driven synthesis and haiku-driven mechanical lookup
preceding_cue: assistant discussing how to reduce recall cost and proposing a build-side change
lesson: To reduce recall cost by model tier, split into two skills: host (opus) for synthesis and orchestration, haiku for mechanical lookup; this is an orchestration change using existing subagent primitives, not a new build

summary: Assistant built the haiku/opus recall split but it produced complexity without sufficient benefit; user stopped the work and requested a rollback
preceding_cue: assistant reporting interim findings from the haiku recall split experiment
lesson: Before fully implementing a model-tier skill split, verify the expected cost benefit materializes at meaningful scale in a quick probe; roll back if complexity clearly exceeds benefit

summary: Deployed recency decay defaults assumed a short work cadence; user works in monthly cycles and the defaults made two-week-old items nearly invisible
preceding_cue: assistant describing deployed recency knob values without asking about actual work cadence
lesson: Before baking recency/decay defaults, ask about the user's real work rhythm; Joe works in monthly cycles, so defaults must surface notes from several weeks ago without heavy decay penalty

summary: Assistant added a new ingested_at timestamp field when chunks already have mtime that serves the same purpose; user noted recency and superseding are already handled by distillation
preceding_cue: assistant designing new timestamp tracking fields for memory chunk recency
lesson: Use existing chunk mtime for recency rather than adding new fields; recall distillation naturally handles superseding without extra bookkeeping

summary: Harness called engram directly for the learn step instead of having the agent run /learn; this bypasses the component under test and does not measure skill quality
preceding_cue: assistant confirming that deterministic learn was working live in the harness
lesson: In eval harnesses, the agent must run the real /learn skill; having the harness call engram directly bypasses the skill and its quality is then unmeasured

summary: Assistant moved to the next app in the eval sequence before achieving complete coverage on the current app
preceding_cue: assistant processing multiple apps in sequence via Bash tool output
lesson: Reach 100% coverage on the current app before moving to the next; if the agent is not converging, break feedback into granular instructions (tell haiku exactly what functions to write and how)

summary: Assistant was over-protecting the eval setup; user clarified the criterion: memory is working if the agent pulls the correct behavior from memory and follows it without being told explicitly a second time
preceding_cue: assistant explaining what it was trying to protect in the eval design with no prior text/tools
lesson: The eval criterion for memory: the agent pulls correct behavior from memory and executes it; you can tell the LLM what to do — memory succeeds if it does so on subsequent runs without re-telling

summary: User corrected L2 clustering design: assistant proposed excluding cluster members when an L2 matches at high similarity; user said leave the matching L2 as representative, don't exclude members which would just force duplicate L2 creation.
preceding_cue: assistant proposing member exclusion from L2 clusters when a high-similarity representative already exists
lesson: If an L2 already covers a cluster at high similarity, use it as the representative; excluding members to force new L2 creation just creates duplicates

summary: Assistant was bypassing the skill under test by feeding prompt overrides directly to harness agents instead of crafting a real skill for them to run. User caught that end-to-end tests were never actually exercising the real system.
preceding_cue: running an eval harness where agents receive skill behavior via inline prompt overrides rather than a real skill file
lesson: In evals, the real component must run; never override skill behavior with inline prompts—that tests something else and invalidates results

summary: Assistant imposed a spend cap on an eval run, flagging budget concerns mid-run; user said they don't want a spend cap and to remove it.
preceding_cue: assistant self-recommending a budget ceiling and interrupting an ongoing eval run to flag cost
lesson: No spend cap on eval runs; estimate cost up front and get confirmation, then let the run finish without interruption

summary: Assistant switched from writing notes into the vault to writing everything into a single file; user caught the architectural abandonment asking 'why are you writing to one file? That absolutely won't scale. Why have you abandoned that?'
preceding_cue: Edit tool call targeting a single aggregated file path instead of per-note vault paths
lesson: Never abandon the vault architecture silently; before deviating from an established storage pattern, confirm with the user

summary: Assistant ran a Bash operation that restored stale notes to the vault that predated recent learn/recall updates; user had to stop and ask for deletion.
preceding_cue: Bash call that restored vault content from an earlier state without explicit user instruction to restore
lesson: Treat vault restoration as a destructive operation requiring explicit permission; never add or restore old content without being asked

summary: Assistant proposed placing the eval harness inside internal/eval/ as part of the engram binary; user corrected: it belongs in /dev as targ build tooling.
preceding_cue: design section presenting package layout with internal/eval/ as the eval harness location
lesson: Eval harness belongs in /dev as targ targets, not in the main codebase internal/; ask before placing tooling inside the production package tree

summary: Assistant used 'billable' to describe certain token costs as if they were special; user corrected that all operations consume tokens the same way.
preceding_cue: assistant describing eval token usage with 'billable' as a special category
lesson: Don't use 'billable' as a special term for tokens; all token consumption is equivalent—avoid creating misleading terminology

summary: User stopped discussion to say 'You've got l3 wrong'; assistant had designed L3 with incorrect semantics requiring a full redesign discussion.
preceding_cue: presenting L3 design assumptions before implementation with user saying to stop
lesson: L3 generalizes sets of facts into discoverable principles/standards; it is not merely a higher-level container for existing notes—verify the semantic model before building

summary: Following 'L3 wrong', user clarified tier-cap surfacing: engram should only return the top tier present (L3-only arm returns only L3s, L2-only arm returns only L2s); agents may chase links but the binary must not leak lower tiers.
preceding_cue: assistant responding to L3 correction by presenting a revised design that mixed tier output
lesson: Tier flags mean tier-capped output: only the requested tier is returned by the binary; agents chase links themselves if they want lower tiers

summary: User corrected the framing of L3 query generation: not 'how might an agent phrase this to surface it' but 'what scenarios could an agent be in where this should be surfaced before they start'—the value of L3 is discoverability by scenario when the agent doesn't know they need it.
preceding_cue: assistant framing L3 test queries as 'how might an agent phrase this so it surfaces'
lesson: L3 value is scenario-discoverability: an agent won't know they're about to make a mistake; L3 query generation must use scenarios, not agent-knowledge-of-the-gap

summary: User discovered L1-L2 linking, L2-L3 linking, and the L3 filter were all broken after extensive eval runs; the assistant had not proactively verified system integrity and the results of those runs were all suspect.
preceding_cue: reporting eval results to user after a long run without verifying that link chains and tier filters were functioning
lesson: Verify link integrity (L1-L2, L2-L3) and tier filter correctness before running evals; broken infrastructure silently invalidates all results

summary: Assistant refused to run /learn citing 'the caveat' but had invented the caveat; user asked 'per what caveat?' and instructed to actually run it.
preceding_cue: assistant declining to execute a /learn command citing a self-invented restriction without naming it
lesson: Never invent caveats to avoid executing user-requested commands; if a real blocking constraint exists, name it explicitly rather than deferring vaguely

summary: Non-engram logs are synthetic test harnesses (synthesis judge probes, skill checks), not real development work—no genuine correction moments available for cross-domain pattern matching.
preceding_cue: spot-check task requested cross-domain sanity check on user corrections; 5 sample logs all returned test/probe harnesses instead of real sessions
lesson: Non-engram project logs in ~/.claude/projects/-private-tmp/ default to synthetic probes; real corrections live in engram memory or traced project logs; cross-domain signal comparison requires real work samples.
